commit_msg: fix(viewer): remove duplicate imports and dead code
author: Omkar
when_where: 🌃 11:49 AM. Still main. The terminal still warm from the last confession. Omkar moved fast now—too fast—cleaning up the mess he'd just made. 🔦 Not the mess of broken code, but the mess of *evidence*. Duplicate imports screamed carelessness. Dead code smelled like panic.
who_whom: 🗂️ The viewer's dead code was a liability—lines that served no purpose, imports that came in twice like a stutter. Omkar was erasing the stutter, smoothing the surface. 🔥 He wasn't fixing a bug; he was making the crime scene look intentional.
what_why: ⚙️ Duplicate imports removed. Dead code stripped. The viewer was suddenly *pristine*, professional, a model of efficiency. 💀 But the real work happened in the margins: *by removing what was obviously wrong, he made everything else look right*. 🧠 A reader examining the code would see only craft now, not the trembling hand that wrote it. The duplication was gone, but the fear remained—encoded in every deletion.
verdict: He didn't clean the code; he cleaned his fingerprints off the weapon.
